mapper.select: SELECT r.id AS resumeId, r.resume_title AS resumeTitle, r.visibility AS visibility, r.is_default AS isDefault, r.status AS status, IFNULL(edu.educationCount, 0) AS educationCount, IFNULL(exp.experienceCount, 0) AS experienceCount, IFNULL(proj.projectCount, 0) AS projectCount, IFNULL(skill.skillCount, 0) AS skillCount, IFNULL(cert.certificateCount, 0) AS certificateCount, CASE WHEN r.contact_email IS NULL OR r.contact_email = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasContactEmail, CASE WHEN r.contact_phone IS NULL OR r.contact_phone = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasContactPhone, CASE WHEN r.career_objective IS NULL OR r.career_objective = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasCareerObjective, CASE WHEN r.self_introduction IS NULL OR r.self_introduction = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasSelfIntroduction FROM education_resume_db.resume r LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS educationCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_education GROUP BY resume_id ) edu ON edu.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS experienceCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_experience GROUP BY resume_id ) exp ON exp.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS projectCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_project GROUP BY resume_id ) proj ON proj.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS skillCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_skill GROUP BY resume_id ) skill ON skill.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS certificateCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_certificate GROUP BY resume_id ) cert ON cert.resume_id = r.id WHERE r.student_id = #{studentId} ORDER BY IFNULL(r.is_default, 0) DESC, r.updated_time DESC, r.created_time DESC, r.id DESC

SELECT r.id AS resumeId, r.student_id AS studentId, r.resume_title AS resumeTitle, r.visibility AS visibility, r.is_default AS isDefault, r.status AS status, r.contact_email AS contactEmail, r.contact_phone AS contactPhone, r.career_objective AS careerObjective, r.self_introduction AS selfIntroduction, IFNULL(skill.skillSummary, '') AS skillSummary, IFNULL(edu.educationCount, 0) AS educationCount, IFNULL(exp.experienceCount, 0) AS experienceCount, IFNULL(proj.projectCount, 0) AS projectCount, IFNULL(skill.skillCount, 0) AS skillCount, IFNULL(cert.certificateCount, 0) AS certificateCount, CASE WHEN r.contact_email IS NULL OR r.contact_email = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasContactEmail, CASE WHEN r.contact_phone IS NULL OR r.contact_phone = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasContactPhone, CASE WHEN r.career_objective IS NULL OR r.career_objective = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasCareerObjective, CASE WHEN r.self_introduction IS NULL OR r.self_introduction = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hasSelfIntroduction FROM education_resume_db.resume r LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS educationCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_education GROUP BY resume_id ) edu ON edu.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS experienceCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_experience GROUP BY resume_id ) exp ON exp.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS projectCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_project GROUP BY resume_id ) proj ON proj.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT rs.resume_id AS resume_id, COUNT(1) AS skillCount, GROUP_CONCAT(rs.skill_name ORDER BY rs.sort_order ASC, rs.created_time ASC SEPARATOR ' / ') AS skillSummary FROM education_resume_db.resume_skill rs GROUP BY rs.resume_id ) skill ON skill.resume_id = r.id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT resume_id, COUNT(1) AS certificateCount FROM education_resume_db.resume_certificate GROUP BY resume_id ) cert ON cert.resume_id = r.id WHERE r.id = #{resumeId} AND r.student_id = #{studentId} LIMIT 1

SELECT c.id AS certificateId, c.certificate_name AS certificateName, c.certificate_number AS certificateNumber, c.status AS status, CASE WHEN c.status IN ('ISSUED', 'ACTIVE', 'VALID') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS verified, c.last_verification_time AS verifiedTime FROM education_resume_db.resume_certificate rc INNER JOIN education_certificate_db.certificate c ON c.id = rc.certificate_id WHERE rc.resume_id = #{resumeId} AND c.student_id = #{studentId} ORDER BY rc.sort_order ASC, rc.id ASC

SELECT ja.student_id AS studentId, COALESCE(u.real_name, u.nickname) AS studentName, s.student_number AS studentNumber, u.avatar AS avatarUrl, edu.major AS major, edu.degree AS degree, edu.school_name AS schoolName FROM job_application ja LEFT JOIN education_user_db.students s ON s.id = ja.student_id LEFT JOIN education_user_db.users u ON u.id = s.user_id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT t.resume_id, t.school_name, t.degree, t.major FROM ( SELECT re.resume_id, re.school_name, re.degree, re.major, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY re.resume_id ORDER BY re.sort_order ASC, re.created_time ASC, re.id ASC) AS rn FROM education_resume_db.resume_education re ) t WHERE t.rn = 1 ) edu ON edu.resume_id = ja.resume_id WHERE ja.id = #{applicationId} AND ja.enterprise_id = #{enterpriseId} LIMIT 1
SELECT status AS status, COUNT(1) AS totalCount FROM job_application WHERE job_id = #{jobId} AND enterprise_id = #{enterpriseId} GROUP BY status
mapper.update: UPDATE job_application SET latest_communication_id = #{latestCommunicationId}, latest_communication_type = #{latestCommunicationType}, latest_communication_time = #{latestCommunicationTime}, read_by_student = #{readByStudent}, read_by_enterprise = #{readByEnterprise}, updated_time = #{updatedTime} WHERE id = #{id}
mapper.select: SELECT e.user_id FROM job_posting jp INNER JOIN education_user_db.enterprises e ON e.id = jp.enterprise_id WHERE jp.id = #{jobId} LIMIT 1
SELECT user_id FROM education_user_db.students WHERE id = #{studentId} LIMIT 1
SELECT id FROM education_user_db.students WHERE user_id = #{userId} LIMIT 1
